title: ArCharacter
description: An Aristotelian character-layer record — a character viewed through the Aristotelian dialect's vocabulary (hamartia, tragic-hero status, cross-dialect identity hook) per design/aristotelian-sketch-01.md A5. Aristotelian does not duplicate character modeling from substrate Entities or Dramatic Characters — `character_ref_id` points at an existing record when one exists; when the Aristotelian encoding stands alone, the ref is None and the dialect carries the character on its own.

id.description: Stable identifier for this character (e.g., 'ar_oedipus'). PFS6-C2.

name.description: Display name for this character (e.g., 'Oedipus'). PFS6-C2.

character_ref_id.description: Optional cross-dialect identity hook. Points at a substrate Entity id or a Dramatic Character id when the encoding authors one; None / absent when the Aristotelian encoding stands alone. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2 / PFS5 OQ3 — cross-dialect audit surface is PFS6 OQ4. PFS6-C3.

hamartia_text.description: Optional author prose describing this character's hamartia — 'missing the mark' per Poetics 1453a, NOT the Bradleyan 'tragic flaw' reading. No length constraint. PFS6-C4.

is_tragic_hero.description: Optional flag distinguishing the central tragic hero from other characters who may also carry a hamartia (e.g., Jocasta in Oedipus). Python default is false; schema admits absence and false interchangeably. PFS6-C5.

anagnorisis_absent.description: A18 (aristotelian-sketch-05). Optional authorial claim that this character is a tragic hero whose hamartia produces catastrophe for others but whose own arc does not contain a recognition moment (e.g., Cordelia in Lear). Python default is false; schema admits absence and false interchangeably. Pre-sketch-05 silence is preserved by the default — the dialect makes no claim about whether a character has an anagnorisis unless the author sets this field true. A7.16 enforces consistency (only on is_tragic_hero=true characters; never on the mythos's main-anagnorisis character; never on characters with chain steps). PFS16-C6.
